description_long: This object is a cluster of slender green stems topped with multiple small, vibrant purple flower spikes, resembling a bouquet of lavender. Each stem is straight and upright, with the flower heads arranged in a loose, vertical grouping. The flower spikes are composed of numerous tiny, closely packed florets, giving a textured and layered appearance. The stems are smooth and cylindrical, with no visible leaves attached along their length. The overall form is delicate and airy, with the purple flower heads providing a striking contrast to the green stems. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative landscaping along sidewalks.
description: A bouquet of slender green stems topped with dense, vibrant purple flower spikes, resembling lavender. The arrangement is delicate, upright, and visually striking.
description_view_0: Several upright green stems with dense purple flower spikes, slightly fanned out and showing the layered florets clearly.
description_view_1: The stems appear more closely grouped, with the purple flower spikes overlapping and forming a compact cluster.
description_view_2: A wider spread of stems is visible, with the purple flower heads more evenly distributed and less overlapping.
description_view_3: The stems and flower spikes are seen from a slightly different angle, showing the vertical alignment and the textured surface of the flower heads.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in dense, whorled clusters along the flower spike, with each floret forming a radial symmetry around the central axis of the spike. The arrangement is layered and overlapping, typical of inflorescences like those of lavender.
